rulings.Case Title: NICOLE GODDARD VS. JANICE FURMAN et al
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, April 30, 2015, Line 4, DEFENDANT YUSEF ABDULLAH'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. Summary judgment granted. Defendant Yusef Mohamed Abdullah met his initial burden presenting evidence that shows that Plaintiff cannot establish that Defendant acted negligently or that any alleged negligence by Defendant was a substantial factor in causing Plaintiff's harm. (Defendant's Undisputed Material Facts ("UMF") 1-5.) Plaintiff does not submit any evidence in opposition. Given that it is undisputed that Defendant was able to stop his vehicle without hitting Plaintiff's vehicle (UMF 4), there is no triable issue of material fact as to whether Defendant was following Plaintiff's vehicle too closely. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and in compliance with Code of Civil Procedure 437c(g) and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: FRANCISCO GUACAMAYA GARCIA VS. CHAMPION STEAM CLEANING, INC A CALIFORNIA CORPORA ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff Francisco Guacamaya Garcias Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Defendant Champion Steam Cleaning, Inc. To Provide Reponses To Plaintiff Form Interrogatories- General, Set One, And Requests For Production Of Documents, Set One And Request For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday April 30, 2015, Line 2, Plaintiff Francisco Guacamaya Garcia's Motion To Compel Defendant Champion Steam Cleaning, Inc. To Provide Reponses To Plaintiff Form Interrogatories- General, Set One, And Requests For Production Of Documents, Set One And Request For Sanctions. Pro Tem Judge Robert Kane, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: It appears that responses have been filed without objection so there is nothing to compel even though the parties are still engaging in the meet and confer process, which if unsuccessful would require filing of a new motion. With respect to sanctions, as there was no opposition to the motion, sanctions are not recoverable under CCP §2031.300(c). With respect to CCP §2023.010(d), sanctions require that there not only be a failure to comply but that it be wilful. (Miranda v. 21st Century Ins. Co. (2004) 117 Cal.App.4th 913, 929.) Based on the fact that defense counsel requested an extension and no response was made by Plaintiff's counsel, the court finds that sanctions are not warranted under this section. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to rkane1089@aol.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: DAVID BAKER ET AL VS. NINA MACKENZIE
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendants Notice Of Motion And Motion To Be Relieved From Default Judgment
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, April 30, 2015, Line 8, Defendant Nina Mackenzie, Personal Representative of the Estate of Peter MacKenzie's Motion To Be Relieved From Default Judgment. The motion is granted based on the discretionary and mandatory portions of CCP 473. Mr. Doolittle demonstrates excusable neglect based on the procedural ambiguity in the case and he also provides an attorney affidavit of fault. The motion was made in a timely fashion, Defendant provides a proposed pleading, and there is a liberal policy to hear cases on the merits. Mr. Doolittle shall remit $500 to Plaintiff by May 15, 2015, as reasonable fees and costs for obtaining the default. rulings.Case Title: WILDA L WHITE VS. GIRISH SUBRAMANYAN MD ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, April 30, 2015, Line 9, DEFENDANTS GIRISH SUBRAMANYAN AND SAN FRANCISCO PSYCHIATRISTS, INC'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. The Court sustains without leave to amend the demurrer to the intentional infliction of emotional distress and negligent infliction of emotional distress causes of action. Both of those claims are based on defendant providing medical records to plaintiff. California Health & Safety Code sec. 123110(h) provides that a "health care provider shall not be liable to the patient or any other person for any consequences that result from disclosure of patient records as required by this chapter." Once a patient such as plaintiff requests her records (Amended Complaint par. 19), defendant's statutory obligation to turn them over and the concomitant immunity from liability exceeds the 15-day response deadline. In addition, the negligent infliction of emotional distress cause of action fails because it is not a distinct claim. Plaintiff's theory of liability based on the ADHD medication and theory of liability based on the formulation note arise out of the same train of events and are part of the medical malpractice cause of action. See Lawson v. Management Activities (1999) 69 Cal.App.4th 652, 656. As noted, however, there can be no liability based on providing the formulation note under the Health & Safety Code. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)
